90: )
91: 
92: // Config is the configuration for the boundary controller
93: type Config struct {
94: 	*configutil.SharedConfig `hcl:"-"`
95: 
96: 	Worker     *Worker     `hcl:"worker"`
97: 	Controller *Controller `hcl:"controller"`
98: 
99: 	// Dev-related options
100: 	DevController        bool   `hcl:"-"`
101: 	PassthroughDirectory string `hcl:"-"`
102: 	DevControllerKey     string `hcl:"-"`
103: 	DevWorkerAuthKey     string `hcl:"-"`
104: 	DevRecoveryKey       string `hcl:"-"`
105: 
106: 	// Eventing configuration for the controller
107: 	Eventing *event.EventerConfig `hcl:"events"`
108: }
109: 
110: type Controller struct {
111: 	Name              string    `hcl:"name"`
112: 	Description       string    `hcl:"description"`
113: 	Database          *Database `hcl:"database"`
114: 	PublicClusterAddr string    `hcl:"public_cluster_addr"`
115: 
116: 	// AuthTokenTimeToLive is the total valid lifetime of a token denoted by time.Duration
117: 	AuthTokenTimeToLive         interface{} `hcl:"auth_token_time_to_live"`
118: 	AuthTokenTimeToLiveDuration time.Duration
119: 
120: 	// AuthTokenTimeToStale is the total time a token can go unused before becoming invalid
121: 	// denoted by time.Duration
122: 	AuthTokenTimeToStale         interface{} `hcl:"auth_token_time_to_stale"`
123: 	AuthTokenTimeToStaleDuration time.Duration
124: 
125: 	// StatusGracePeriod represents the period of time (as a duration) that the
126: 	// controller will wait before marking connections from a disconnected worker
127: 	// as invalid.
128: 	//
129: 	// TODO: This field is currently internal.
130: 	StatusGracePeriodDuration time.Duration `hcl:"-"`
131: }
132: 
133: func (c *Controller) InitNameIfEmpty() (string, error) {
134: 	if c == nil {
135: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("controller config is empty")
136: 	}
137: 	if err := initNameIfEmpty(&c.Name); err != nil {
138: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("error auto-generating controller name: %w", err)
139: 	}
140: 	return c.Name, nil
141: }
142: 
143: type Worker struct {
144: 	Name        string   `hcl:"name"`
145: 	Description string   `hcl:"description"`
146: 	Controllers []string `hcl:"controllers"`
147: 	PublicAddr  string   `hcl:"public_addr"`
148: 
149: 	// We use a raw interface for parsing so that people can use JSON-like
150: 	// syntax that maps directly to the filter input or possibly more familiar
151: 	// key=value syntax. This is trued up in the Parse function below.
152: 	TagsRaw interface{}         `hcl:"tags"`
153: 	Tags    map[string][]string `hcl:"-"`
154: 
155: 	// StatusGracePeriod represents the period of time (as a duration) that the
156: 	// worker will wait before disconnecting connections if it cannot make a
157: 	// status report to a controller.
158: 	//
159: 	// TODO: This field is currently internal.
160: 	StatusGracePeriodDuration time.Duration `hcl:"-"`
161: }
162: 
163: func (w *Worker) InitNameIfEmpty() (string, error) {
164: 	if w == nil {
165: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("worker config is empty")
166: 	}
167: 	if err := initNameIfEmpty(&w.Name); err != nil {
168: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("error auto-generating worker name: %w", err)
169: 	}
170: 	return w.Name, nil
171: }
172: 
173: func initNameIfEmpty(name *string) error {
174: 	if *name == "" {
175: 		var err error
176: 		if *name, err = base62.Random(10); err != nil {
177: 			return err
178: 		}
179: 	}
180: 	return nil
181: }
182: 
183: type Database struct {
184: 	Url                string `hcl:"url"`
185: 	MigrationUrl       string `hcl:"migration_url"`
186: 	MaxOpenConnections int    `hcl:"max_open_connections"`
187: }
188: 
189: // DevWorker is a Config that is used for dev mode of Boundary
190: // workers
191: func DevWorker() (*Config, error) {
192: 	parsed, err := Parse(devConfig + devWorkerExtraConfig)
193: 	if err != nil {
194: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing dev config: %w", err)
195: 	}
196: 	return parsed, nil
197: }
198: 
199: func DevKeyGeneration() (string, string, string) {
200: 	var numBytes int64 = 96
201: 	randBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)
202: 	n, err := randBuf.ReadFrom(&io.LimitedReader{
203: 		R: rand.Reader,
204: 		N: numBytes,
205: 	})
206: 	if err != nil {
207: 		panic(err)
208: 	}
209: 	if n != numBytes {
210: 		panic(fmt.Errorf("expected to read 64 bytes, read %d", n))
211: 	}
212: 	controllerKey := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(randBuf.Bytes()[0:32])
213: 	workerAuthKey := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(randBuf.Bytes()[32:64])
214: 	recoveryKey := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(randBuf.Bytes()[64:numBytes])
215: 
216: 	return controllerKey, workerAuthKey, recoveryKey
217: }
218: 
219: // DevController is a Config that is used for dev mode of Boundary
220: // controllers
221: func DevController() (*Config, error) {
222: 	controllerKey, workerAuthKey, recoveryKey := DevKeyGeneration()
223: 
224: 	hclStr := fmt.Sprintf(devConfig+devControllerExtraConfig, controllerKey, workerAuthKey, recoveryKey)
225: 	parsed, err := Parse(hclStr)
226: 	if err != nil {
227: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing dev config: %w", err)
228: 	}
229: 	parsed.DevController = true
230: 	parsed.DevControllerKey = controllerKey
231: 	parsed.DevWorkerAuthKey = workerAuthKey
232: 	parsed.DevRecoveryKey = recoveryKey
233: 	return parsed, nil
234: }
235: 
236: func DevCombined() (*Config, error) {
237: 	controllerKey, workerAuthKey, recoveryKey := DevKeyGeneration()
238: 	hclStr := fmt.Sprintf(devConfig+devControllerExtraConfig+devWorkerExtraConfig, controllerKey, workerAuthKey, recoveryKey)
239: 	parsed, err := Parse(hclStr)
240: 	if err != nil {
241: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing dev config: %w", err)
242: 	}
243: 	parsed.DevController = true
244: 	parsed.DevControllerKey = controllerKey
245: 	parsed.DevWorkerAuthKey = workerAuthKey
246: 	parsed.DevRecoveryKey = recoveryKey
247: 	return parsed, nil
248: }
249: 
250: func New() *Config {
251: 	return &Config{
252: 		SharedConfig: new(configutil.SharedConfig),
253: 	}
254: }
255: 
256: // LoadFile loads the configuration from the given file.
257: func LoadFile(path string, wrapper wrapping.Wrapper) (*Config, error) {
258: 	d, err := ioutil.ReadFile(path)
259: 	if err != nil {
260: 		return nil, err
261: 	}
262: 
263: 	raw := string(d)
264: 
265: 	if wrapper != nil {
266: 		raw, err = configutil.EncryptDecrypt(raw, true, true, wrapper)
267: 		if err != nil {
268: 			return nil, err
269: 		}
270: 	}
271: 
272: 	return Parse(raw)
273: }
274: 
275: func Parse(d string) (*Config, error) {
276: 	obj, err := hcl.Parse(d)
277: 	if err != nil {
278: 		return nil, err
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	result := New()
282: 	if err := hcl.DecodeObject(result, obj); err != nil {
283: 		return nil, err
284: 	}
285: 
286: 	// Perform controller configuration overrides for auth token settings
287: 	if result.Controller != nil {
288: 		result.Controller.Name, err = parseutil.ParsePath(result.Controller.Name)
289: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
290: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing controller name: %w", err)
291: 		}
292: 		if result.Controller.Name != strings.ToLower(result.Controller.Name) {
293: 			return nil, errors.New("Controller name must be all lower-case")
294: 		}
295: 		if !strutil.Printable(result.Controller.Name) {
296: 			return nil, errors.New("Controller name contains non-printable characters")
297: 		}
298: 		if result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToLive != "" {
299: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToLive)
300: 			if err != nil {
301: 				return result, err
302: 			}
303: 			result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToLiveDuration = t
304: 		}
305: 
306: 		if result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToStale != "" {
307: 			t, err := parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToStale)
308: 			if err != nil {
309: 				return result, err
310: 			}
311: 			result.Controller.AuthTokenTimeToStaleDuration = t
312: 		}
313: 	}
314: 
315: 	// Parse worker tags
316: 	if result.Worker != nil {
317: 		result.Worker.Name, err = parseutil.ParsePath(result.Worker.Name)
318: 		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, parseutil.ErrNotAUrl) {
319: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error parsing worker name: %w", err)
320: 		}
321: 		if result.Worker.Name != strings.ToLower(result.Worker.Name) {
322: 			return nil, errors.New("Worker name must be all lower-case")
323: 		}
324: 		if !strutil.Printable(result.Worker.Name) {
325: 			return nil, errors.New("Worker name contains non-printable characters")
326: 		}
327: 		if result.Worker.TagsRaw != nil {
328: 			switch t := result.Worker.TagsRaw.(type) {
329: 			// HCL allows multiple labeled blocks with the same name, turning it
330: 			// into a slice of maps, hence the slice here. This format is the
331: 			// one that ends up matching the JSON that we use in the expression.
332: 			case []map[string]interface{}:
333: 				if err := mapstructure.WeakDecode(t, &result.Worker.Tags); err != nil {
334: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error decoding the worker's %q section: %w", "tags", err)
335: 				}
336: 
337: 			// However for those that are used to other systems, we also accept
338: 			// key=value pairs
339: 			case []interface{}:
340: 				var strs []string
341: 				if err := mapstructure.WeakDecode(t, &strs); err != nil {
342: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error decoding the worker's %q section: %w", "tags", err)
343: 				}
344: 				result.Worker.Tags = make(map[string][]string, len(strs))
345: 				// Aggregate the values by key. We care about the first equal
346: 				// sign only, to allow equals to be in values if needed. This
347: 				// also means we don't support equal signs in keys.
348: 				for _, str := range strs {
349: 					splitStr := strings.SplitN(str, "=", 2)
350: 					switch len(splitStr) {
351: 					case 1:
352: 						return nil, fmt.Errorf("Error decoding tag %q from string: must be in key = value format", str)
353: 					case 2:
354: 						key := splitStr[0]
355: 						v := result.Worker.Tags[key]
356: 						if len(v) == 0 {
357: 							v = make([]string, 0, 1)
358: 						}
359: 						result.Worker.Tags[key] = append(v, splitStr[1])
360: 					}
361: 				}
362: 			}
363: 		}
364: 		for k, v := range result.Worker.Tags {
365: 			if k != strings.ToLower(k) {
366: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag key %q is not all lower-case letters", k)
367: 			}
368: 			if !strutil.Printable(k) {
369: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag key %q contains non-printable characters", k)
370: 			}
371: 			for _, val := range v {
372: 				if val != strings.ToLower(val) {
373: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag value %q for tag key %q is not all lower-case letters", val, k)
374: 				}
375: 				if !strutil.Printable(k) {
376: 					return nil, fmt.Errorf("Tag value %q for tag key %q contains non-printable characters", v, k)
377: 				}
378: 			}
379: 		}
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	sharedConfig, err := configutil.ParseConfig(d)
383: 	if err != nil {
384: 		return nil, err
385: 	}
386: 	result.SharedConfig = sharedConfig
387: 
388: 	for _, listener := range result.SharedConfig.Listeners {
389: 		if strutil.StrListContains(listener.Purpose, "api") &&
390: 			(listener.CorsDisableDefaultAllowedOriginValues == nil || !*listener.CorsDisableDefaultAllowedOriginValues) {
391: 			switch listener.CorsEnabled {
392: 			case nil:
393: 				// If CORS wasn't specified, enable default value of *, which allows
394: 				// both the admin UI (without the user having to explicitly set an
395: 				// origin) and the desktop origin
396: 				listener.CorsEnabled = new(bool)
397: 				*listener.CorsEnabled = true
398: 				listener.CorsAllowedOrigins = []string{"*"}
399: 
400: 			default:
401: 				// If not the wildcard and they haven't disabled us auto-adding
402: 				// origin values, add the desktop client origin
403: 				if *listener.CorsEnabled &&
404: 					!strutil.StrListContains(listener.CorsAllowedOrigins, "*") {
405: 					listener.CorsAllowedOrigins = strutil.AppendIfMissing(listener.CorsAllowedOrigins, desktopCorsOrigin)
406: 				}
407: 			}
408: 		}
409: 	}
410: 
411: 	list, ok := obj.Node.(*ast.ObjectList)
412: 	if !ok {
413: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error parsing: file doesn't contain a root object")
414: 	}
415: 
416: 	eventList := list.Filter("events")
417: 	switch len(eventList.Items) {
418: 	case 0:
419: 		result.Eventing = event.DefaultEventerConfig()
420: 	case 1:
421: 		if result.Eventing, err = parseEventing(eventList.Items[0]); err != nil {
422: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf(`error parsing "events": %w`, err)
423: 		}
424: 	default:
425: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(`too many "events" nodes (max 1, got %d)`, len(eventList.Items))
426: 	}
427: 
428: 	return result, nil
429: }
430: 
431: func parseEventing(eventObj *ast.ObjectItem) (*event.EventerConfig, error) {
432: 	// Decode the outside struct
433: 	var result event.EventerConfig
434: 	if err := hcl.DecodeObject(&result, eventObj.Val); err != nil {
435: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(`error decoding "events" node: %w`, err)
436: 	}
437: 	// Now, find the sinks
438: 	eventObjType, ok := eventObj.Val.(*ast.ObjectType)
439: 	if !ok {
440: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(`error interpreting "events" node as an object type`)
441: 	}
442: 	list := eventObjType.List
443: 	sinkList := list.Filter("sink")
444: 	// Go through each sink and decode
445: 	for i, item := range sinkList.Items {
446: 		var s event.SinkConfig
447: 		if err := hcl.DecodeObject(&s, item.Val); err != nil {
448: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error decoding eventer sink entry %d", i)
449: 		}
450: 
451: 		// Fix up type and validate
452: 		switch {
453: 		case s.Type != "":
454: 		case len(item.Keys) == 1:
455: 			s.Type = event.SinkType(item.Keys[0].Token.Value().(string))
456: 		default:
457: 			switch {
458: 			case s.StderrConfig != nil:
459: 				// If we haven't found the type any other way, they _must_
460: 				// specify this block even though there are no config parameters
461: 				s.Type = event.StderrSink
462: 			case s.FileConfig != nil:
463: 				s.Type = event.FileSink
464: 			default:
465: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("sink type could not be determined")
466: 			}
467: 		}
468: 		s.Type = event.SinkType(strings.ToLower(string(s.Type)))
469: 
470: 		if s.Type == event.StderrSink && s.StderrConfig == nil {
471: 			// StderrConfig is optional as it has no values, but ensure it's
472: 			// always populated if it's the type
473: 			s.StderrConfig = new(event.StderrSinkTypeConfig)
474: 		}
475: 
476: 		// parse the duration string specified in a file config into a time.Duration
477: 		if s.FileConfig != nil && s.FileConfig.RotateDurationHCL != "" {
478: 			var err error
479: 			s.FileConfig.RotateDuration, err = parseutil.ParseDurationSecond(s.FileConfig.RotateDurationHCL)
480: 			if err != nil {
481: 				return nil, fmt.Errorf("can't parse rotation duration %s", s.FileConfig.RotateDurationHCL)
482: 			}
483: 		}
484: 
485: 		if err := s.Validate(); err != nil {
486: 			return nil, err
487: 		}
488: 
489: 		// Append to result
490: 		result.Sinks = append(result.Sinks, &s)
491: 	}
492: 	if len(result.Sinks) == 0 {
493: 		result.Sinks = []*event.SinkConfig{event.DefaultSink()}
494: 	}
495: 	return &result, nil
496: }
497: 
498: // Sanitized returns a copy of the config with all values that are considered
499: // sensitive stripped. It also strips all `*Raw` values that are mainly
500: // used for parsing.
501: //
502: // Specifically, the fields that this method strips are:
503: // - KMS.Config
504: // - Telemetry.CirconusAPIToken
505: func (c *Config) Sanitized() map[string]interface{} {
506: 	// Create shared config if it doesn't exist (e.g. in tests) so that map
507: 	// keys are actually populated
508: 	if c.SharedConfig == nil {
509: 		c.SharedConfig = new(configutil.SharedConfig)
510: 	}
511: 	sharedResult := c.SharedConfig.Sanitized()
512: 	result := map[string]interface{}{}
513: 	for k, v := range sharedResult {
514: 		result[k] = v
515: 	}
516: 
517: 	return result
518: }

(End of file - total 518 lines)

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: //go:build ui
2: // +build ui
3: 
4: package controller
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"net/http"
8: 	"strings"
9: 
10: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/ui"
11: )
12: 
13: func init() {
14: 	handleUi = handleUiWithAssets
15: }
16: 
17: func handleUiWithAssets(c *Controller) http.Handler {
18: 	var nextHandler http.Handler
19: 	if c.conf.RawConfig.PassthroughDirectory != "" {
20: 		nextHandler = devPassthroughHandler(c.conf.RawConfig.PassthroughDirectory)
21: 	} else {
22: 		nextHandler = ui.Handler()
23: 	}
24: 
25: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
26: 		if r.Method != http.MethodGet {
27: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)
28: 			return
29: 		}
30: 
31: 		dotIndex := strings.LastIndex(r.URL.Path, ".")
32: 		switch dotIndex {
33: 		case -1:
34: 			// For all paths without an extension serve /index.html
35: 			r.URL.Path = "/"
36: 
37: 		default:
38: 			switch r.URL.Path {
39: 			case "/", "/favicon.png", "/assets/styles.css":
40: 
41: 			default:
42: 				for i := dotIndex + 1; i < len(r.URL.Path); i++ {
43: 					intVal := r.URL.Path[i]
44: 					// Current guidance from FE is if it's only alphanum after
45: 					// the last dot, treat it as an extension
46: 					if intVal < '0' ||
47: 						(intVal > '9' && intVal < 'A') ||
48: 						(intVal > 'Z' && intVal < 'a') ||
49: 						intVal > 'z' {
50: 						// Not an extension. Serve the contents of index.html
51: 						r.URL.Path = "/"
52: 					}
53: 				}
54: 			}
55: 		}
56: 
57: 		// Fall through to the next handler
58: 		nextHandler.ServeHTTP(w, r)
59: 	})
60: }

(End of file - total 60 lines)
</content>

1: package controller
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"net/http"
6: 	"path/filepath"
7: 
8: 	"github.com/hashicorp/boundary/internal/observability/event"
9: )
10: 
11: func devPassthroughHandler(passthroughDir string) http.Handler {
12: 	const op = "controller.devPassthroughHandler"
13: 	ctx := context.TODO()
14: 	// Panic may not be ideal but this is never a production call and it'll
15: 	// panic on startup. We could also just change the function to return
16: 	// an error.
17: 	abs, err := filepath.Abs(passthroughDir)
18: 	if err != nil {
19: 		panic(err)
20: 	}
21: 	event.WriteSysEvent(ctx, op, "serving passthrough files at /", "path", abs)
22: 	fs := http.FileServer(http.Dir(abs))
23: 	prefixHandler := http.StripPrefix("/", fs)
24: 
25: 	return prefixHandler
26: }
27: 
28: var handleUi = func(c *Controller) http.Handler {
29: 	if c.conf.RawConfig.PassthroughDirectory != "" {
30: 		return devPassthroughHandler(c.conf.RawConfig.PassthroughDirectory)
31: 	}
32: 	return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
33: 		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
34: 	})
35: }

(End of file - total 35 lines)
